setting.note: Hlynur Björn, a thirtyish layabout who still lives with his mother, drifts through the bars, screens and hangovers of downtown Reykjavík, his aimless world upended when his mother's lover moves in. Helgason maps the real 101 postal district that gives the book its name: the flat in the old centre, the shopping and bar street of Laugavegur, the downtown pubs of the weekend rúntur, and the little lake of Tjörnin at the city's heart.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Hallgrímur Helgason, first published 1996. Mapped as a cited work: no text stored, no passages reproduced. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: 101 Reykjavík, Hallgrímur Helgason (1996). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The cold blue of the long Reykjavík winter dark, the backdrop to Hlynur's screen-lit, bar-lit drift. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
